questions.question: How does `defineModel` expose custom modifiers to the component's logic?
options: As a second element in the destructured tuple: `const [model, modifiers] = defineModel()`
As a prop named `modelModifiers` that must be declared with `defineProps`
As a parameter injected automatically into the `setup` function

questions.question: What is a known gotcha of the built-in `.number` modifier on native inputs?
options: It parses using `parseInt` instead of `parseFloat`, losing decimal values
It returns an empty string (not `0`) when the input is cleared, and `parseFloat('123abc')` returns `123`
It requires `type="number"` on the input or it has no effect
It conflicts with `.trim` when both are applied to the same input

questions.question: Where does custom modifier logic run when using `defineModel` with a `set` transform?
options: In the parent component before the value is bound to the child prop
Inside the child component's `set` function before emitting `update:modelValue`
In a Vue compiler plugin that transforms the template at build time
In the parent's `v-model` directive handler after the child emits
